name: devsecops-agency
description: A fully autonomous AI software-development company in a plugin. You speak only to the CEO; the CEO holds board meetings with Chiefs, each of whom runs a council of specialists. Research → Product → Architecture → Security → Execution → Quality → DevOps → Docs → Legal → Marketing → Strategy → People-ops → Audit. Security-first (STRIDE + OWASP Top 10) by default. Ships to GitHub. v0.2 adds: durable cross-project memory (three-tier Light/Deep/REM dreaming) + append-only JSONL session logs, scoped AGENTS.md hierarchy + deterministic ordering, gates (green/yellow/red/n/a with blocking-vs-informing councils), taskflow (six-state machine + 2-attempt fix-loop cap), worktree parallelism with atomic scope-checked merge, skill-creator runtime roster extension, per-agent model tiering (Opus CEO / Sonnet Chiefs / Haiku specialists), rate-limited notify, and novelty-gated memory writes. v0.3.0 'company release': Wave 1 adds durable corporate paper trail — `vision-doc` (workspace VISION.md + 3-bullet KR slice per dispatch), `okr` (per-project OKR derivation + per-report alignment + quarter roll-up), `adr` (immutable decision receipts), `meeting-minutes` (minutes for every user/board/blocking-council/red-team/audit/retro convening). Wave 2 adds Marketing + Strategy councils (CMO + CSO + 8 specialists) plus four new skills: `idea-pipeline` (4-stage gated ideation → top-5), `user-meeting` (4-phase CEO↔user selection flow with live Cowork artifact), `market-intel` (canonical artifact shapes), `positioning` (messaging canvas + narrative rubric). Wave 3 adds People-ops + Audit councils (COO + CAO + 7 specialists: roster-manager, hiring-lead, performance-reviewer, adr-auditor, gate-auditor, okr-auditor, memory-auditor) plus three new skills: `roster` (living agent registry + hire/fire/tier-change/repurpose lifecycle with mandatory ADRs and `_vision/roster/_archive/`), `audit` (independent close + portfolio paper-trail integrity, 4 audit kinds, every red → ADR), `capacity` (per-agent + per-council utilization bands + KR coverage gaps). Wave 4 adds the never-give-up resilience ladder — `ladder` skill with 8 rungs (0 Retry → 1 Fix-loop → 2 Alternate approach → 3 Cross-council → 4 Hire/repurpose via COO → 5 Scope pivot → 6 User consult → 7 Parking lot), per-rung attempt budgets, ADR per transition, Rung 7 as resumable terminal state that preserves every artifact with a reconsider-trigger. Taskflow extended with `ladderRung` field; gates reds start the ladder instead of durably blocking. Waves 5–7 add eval + benchmarks + budget, red-team + self-modifying playbooks, and SRE + tool-scout + provenance.
